entry.title: Re: Negative value not allowed for depreciation area 93
entry.content: Thank you Mark for confirming my thoughts. We have tried changing the other depr areas to allow negative signs (in Additional Parameters) but with no success to achieve the wanted outcome. Any other ideas?

entry.title: How to match the numbers in "Users Authorized to Debug / Replace" in EWA report
entry.content:
We would like to understand how the EWA report is calculating this authorization checks for "Users Authorized to Debug / Replace" in EWA report.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to match the numbers in "Users Authorized to Debu...
entry.content:
We can see the below content in EWA report .
=================
11.2.5.1.2 Users Authorized to Debug / Replace
Authorization objects: Object 1: S_DEVELOP with ACTVT=02 (change) and OBJTYPE=DEBUG
=================

The checks should be performed via SUIM.
Considering that duplicate names are removed and only unlocked users are considered.

Steps:
Navigate to transaction SUIM - Expand User - Expand Users by Complex Selection Criteria - Execute Users by Complex Selection Criteria
Enter Object 1: S_DEVELOP with ACTVT=02 (change) and OBJTYPE=DEBUG, select "Only users without locks".
And then match the numbers with the number in EWA report.

entry.title: Work Center Auto Update in PM Order Confirmation
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I wanted to know if it is possible to set the work center to auto update when a confirmation is performed.It should assume the work center of the person that performed the actual work and not the planned work center. 

One can plan for a certain skill level but work gets performed by a different one.

there is no direct interface in my knowledge, which means you need an alternative solution or middleware to replace Oracle.

For SCIM API, could you please clarify why it seems not working for you?

Best regards,

Sirui

entry.title: Re: SAP Marketing Cloud Release 2402 is Here!
entry.content: @andreasfranz

entry.title: Re: Configuring BTP Destination in Advanced Event Mesh fo...
entry.content:
If I understand your setup correctly, you are using the AEM REST Delivery Point feature to have AEM push the events to CAP? This requires indeed quite some configuration in AEM and (imho) creates a quite tight coupling between AEM and your CAP app. And no, there is no destination support inside AEM.

While it is a valid approach and will work great, I tend to approach the connectivity in the opposite direction, from CAP to AEM (or any other messaging option such as the Event Mesh of SAP Integration Suite or the SAP Cloud Application Event Hub).

Have a look at the following sample repository containing an example for both EM and AEM: https://github.com/willempardaens/cap-eventing. You will notice that it is the CAP application taking the initiative to connect/listen to AEM. The credentials to connect to AEM can come from a service binding to avoid hard-coding.

At this point in time CAP does not have native support for AEM, but the team is working on it (see official roadmap item: https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?q=advanced%252520event%252520mesh&range=CURRENT-LAST#;INNO=0D8AD41AB0071EDF95844C515718CA24), so for now you can use the sample code to explore the overall approach and POC, and switch to the native support once available.

Also have a look at @ajmaradiaga 's recent eBook on the same topic: https://www.sap-press.com/developing-event-driven-integrations-with-sap-btp_6021/

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)的初级架构理解
entry.content:
自己属于Open系开发，目前刚刚着手计划转型SAP-BTP，感谢分享！！！

让自己对于SAP-BTP有了一个初步了解。

entry.title: Consume ABAP RFC Function Modules in CAP
entry.content:
Introduction

It has been possible for some time now to use ABAP RFC function modules in CAP applications. This provides a convenient way to integrate with existing ABAP functionality without needing to create a custom OData API when a standard one is unavailable. By leveraging standard RFC function modules, you can avoid unnecessary custom development in your backend system.

In this blog post, I’ll show you how to configure Build Code and VSCode to use ABAP RFC function modules.

However, use this approach carefully and only when necessary. I still recommend using APIs whenever possible—resorting to RFCs should be your last option.

Prerequisite

To try this out, I set up my own ABAP Developer system. You can do the same by downloading and installing it as described in the official documentation: https://hub.docker.com/r/sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial

Download docker image

docker pull sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRIAL_2022_SP01

Create a docker container:

docker run --stop-timeout 3600 -it --name a4h -h vhcala4hci -p 3200:3200 -p 3300:3300 -p 8443:8443 -p 30213:30213 -p 50000:50000 -p 50001:50001 sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRIAL_2022_SP01 --skip-limits-check

Start ABAP system:

docker start -ai a4h

Start cloud connector:

docker exec -it a4h bash 
/usr/local/sbin/rcscc_daemon start

Configure Cloud Connector (only for Build Code)

The configuration of the cloud connector is described here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/build_code/d0d8f5bfc3d640478854e6f4e7c7584a/892114ce078b4e17a9ff7e751e6330cc.html#explore-sap-system-functions

With more details on this page, including principal propagation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/configure-access-control-rfc

Since my ABAP Developer Edition is not fully configured, I opted for a simplified setup using a technical user.

In the Cloud Connector, I configured access to the ABAP system using its IP address:

In the list of recources, I defined all Function modules I wanted to use:

Overall, it should look like this:

BTP Destination (only for Build Code)

In BTP, I configured the following destination using the virtual hostname, authenticated with my own user. Below is the destination configuration:

 Build Code

Start by creating a new Full Stack Application in Build Code. If the following functionality is not available immediately, you may need to recreate the project and developer space after setting up the destination. (In my case, I had to recreate the project and space after the destination was created.)

Go to the storyboard and add a new external resource:

Here, you’ll now have the option “Function”  and you’ll find the earlier created destination:

Now, you can start searching for Function Modules to use in your CAP project:

Once you found the Function Module, you can add it to your project.

This will also request to fill in a username and password for the npm repository, which you can create here: https://ui.repositories.cloud.sap/www/webapp/users

You need to take the NPM Base64 Credentials:

This will give you a base64 string which you cannot use in the login page in Build Code. I skipped this and just pasted the NPM Base64 credentials in the .npmrc file:

From here on, you can create a service

I copied the definition of the CDS for the service from the sample repository created by  : https://github.com/gregorwolf/cap-rfc/blob/main/srv/UserService.cds

To use the Function Module, you need to implement the following logic behind the CDS definition. I took this example from Gregor’s sample repository: https://github.com/gregorwolf/cap-rfc/blob/main/srv/UserService.js

 

Ready to start! This might still lead to the following error:

You can solve it by running “npm i -D @SAP-rfc/node-rfc-library“. If this fails, it means that the config in .npmrc is not correct or the user does not have access to the npm repository.

Once installed, your application should run! 

VSCode

Create a new CAP project using CDS CLI as described in the CAP documentation:  https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/tools/cds-cli#cds-init

Also for VSCode you need to configure the credentials to the npm repository for the rfc library:

Once that’s in place, you can install the rfc library:

Additionally, you have to configure the system in the .env file using the same properties like for the destination in BTP:

Use the following command to import an ABAP Function Module:

Provide the same cds and implementation for the service and it will run just like in Build Code.

Additionally

 In this blog post, I used my own user to connect from CAP to my SAP system. However, for a productive scenario, a technical user won’t be sufficient, and principal propagation will be required. As the next step, I plan to configure principal propagation. In the meantime, feel free to try it yourself by changing the authentication type of the destination to PrincipalPropagation:

 
And follow the config here: https://help.sap.com/docs/connectivity/sap-btp-connectivity-cf/configure-access-control-rfc

 

Conclusion

Everything is possible—but remember, with great power comes great responsibility. Use this approach wisely and only when truly necessary.

Special thanks for Gregor Wolf who already made a sample repository on GitHub: https://github.com/gregorwolf/cap-rfc

entry.title: Re: Pipeline Generic Step01 - Inbound Processing for XI [...
entry.content:
this is a bug of the handled by integration flow feature of the XI adapter, I have raised an incident ticket and development promised to patch the tenants soon, as long as you face the error, you may revert back the version of the XI inbound flow to version 1.0.2, then however you can use the XI end point only for async messages and not for sync, for handling both we had to switch to the handled by integration flow feature

Alex

Alex

entry.title: Re: How to Troubleshoot a HANA Live Report Performance Is...
entry.content:
Hello. To address the performance issues in your CDS view with nested aggregations in a production environment, follow these structured steps:

1. Analyze Execution Plan for Bottlenecks

  • Identify Full Scans: Use HANA Studio's EXPLAIN PLAN to pinpoint large tables (e.g., MATDOC) undergoing full scans. Check if filters (e.g., date ranges) are applied early in the plan.

  • Index Utilization: Ensure indexes on filtered fields (date, plant, material type) are used. If not, verify predicate formats match indexed columns (e.g., avoid functions on indexed fields like TO_DATE(Field)).

2. Optimize Aggregations and Joins

  • Push Aggregations Down: Perform aggregations (e.g., @Aggregation.default: #SUM) at the lowest possible level (e.g., on MATDOC first) before joining with VBAP/Z-tables to reduce intermediate result sizes.

  • Use CE Functions: Replace SQL joins/aggregations with HANA-specific Calculation Engine (CE) functions (e.g., CE_JOIN, CE_AGGREGATION) for optimized execution:

    sql
    Copy
    CE_JOIN(CE_AGGREGATION(MATDOC, [...]), VBAP, [...]).

  • Avoid Nested Aggregations: Break complex aggregations into separate views to force materialization points and prevent redundant calculations.

3. Filter Handling and Client Management

  • Explicit Client Handling: Ensure all joins include MANDT (client) fields to avoid cross-client data scans:

    sql
    Copy
    ON matdoc.mandt = vbap.mandt AND ...

  • Filter Pushdown: Use $parameters.<filter> in CDS to enforce early filter application. Combine with @Optimization.Filter.requiredFields to guide the optimizer.

4. Column Store and Partitioning

  • Column Store: Confirm MATDOC and other large tables are stored in columnar format (transaction SE11 > Technical Settings). Migrate row-store tables if necessary.

  • Partitioning: Partition MATDOC by date (e.g., range partitioning on BUDAT) to enable partition pruning during date-range queries.

5. Authorization and Analytic Privileges

  • Test Without Authorization: Temporarily disable CDS authorization checks (e.g., @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_ALLOWED) to rule out overhead from authorization logic.

  • Simplify Analytic Privileges: If analytic privileges are applied, ensure they use static filters (e.g., fixed plant codes) rather than dynamic joins.

6. HANA-Specific Optimizations

  • Materialized Views: Pre-aggregate MATDOC data using calculation views or SQL-based materialized views for frequently queried fields (e.g., daily sums).

  • Update Statistics: Run UPDATE STATISTICS for tables involved to ensure the optimizer has accurate cardinality estimates.

7. Testing and Validation

  • Simulate Production Data in DEV: Copy a production data subset to DEV to replicate the issue. Use HANA Studio's SQL tracer (ST05) to capture the runtime query and analyze it with EXPLAIN PLAN.

  • Test Incremental Changes: Apply optimizations incrementally (e.g., pushdown aggregation first, then CE functions) to measure individual impacts.

Example CDS Adjustment:

abap
Copy
@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZMATDOC_AGG'
define view ZMATDOC_AGG as select from matdoc {
  key mandt,
  key matnr,
  @Aggregation.default: #SUM
  sum(menge) as total_menge,
  budat
} group by mandt, matnr, budat;

Use this pre-aggregated view in your main CDS to reduce join complexity.

Focus on reducing data volume early via filter pushdown, proper aggregation structuring, and HANA-specific optimizations (CE functions, column store). Validate execution plans for index usage and client handling. Test rigorously with production-like data to ensure scalability.

entry.title: Re: Hi Priyanka,Just like UPDATE_INSERT, Do SAP CPI suppo...
entry.content: Yes, you can do so, but since the function UPDATE_INSERT can handle the checks on its own, the above steps will not be required

entry.title: req.user.id does not give me my user email id from SAP Build Workzone tile.
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am trying to get the logged-in user from my SAP CAPM entity's userDetails to determine the data based on the user email ID. But when reading the req.user.id in workzone, I get 'system' as the logged-in user.

From the SAP Build Workzone tile - > req.user.id = 'system'

From SAP BAS connected with XSUAA binding, I get the - > req.user.id = ''srikar@gmail.com

Is there anything I have to do to get the logged-in user in server.js or a custom JS handler?

I opened Workzone and authenticated from the custom IDP from the organization using email. I need to validate the email in the SAP CAPM service. 

Any thoughts?

Thank You,

Srikar N

entry.title: Re: Unable to Retrieve Business Data in Custom Inbound AP...
entry.content:
Hi ArekA41,

please check the documentation for Providing Access to a Business Service That Consumes Released Objects.

The steps in the tutorial are done in the development system/tenant where you can use the SAP_BR_DEVELOPER role (template) to test a new custom service immediately without having to create an IAM app and assign it to a business catalog and a business role.

When using the custom service in a different system/tenant some additional steps are required as described in the documentation.

Regards,
Peter

entry.title: SAC – Integrated Financial Planning – Data model and calculation method
entry.content:
SAC – Data model and calculation method – specific values of dimension property: DRIVER_INPUT, CLOSING_INPUT and CHANGING_INPUT

Hello SAP-Community,

In the sample End-to-End Planning Process in Integrated Financial Planning for SAP S/4HANA we find the process step “Plan Balance Sheet” 

In this process step we find two Balance Sheets – Changing and Closing. From our understanding the business logic should be: Opening Balance Sheet + Changing Balance Sheet = Closing Balance Sheet.

This logic is reflected within each of those Balance Sheets.

Looking in the data model the only difference between the two Balance Sheets is the filter for the Calc Method property belonging to the dimension ‘SAP_FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT’ in the data model ‘SAP_FI_IFP_IM_FinancialStatement’. The changing and closing balance sheets are filtered by dimension property values CHANGING_INPUT and CLOSING_INPUT, respectively.

                                        

In the data model, we can see that the aforementioned G/L account dimension has members which have different Calc Method values. It means that only G/L accounts with the CHANGING_INPUT Calc Method property are shown in the changing sheet, whereas only the CLOSING_INPUT G/L accounts are shown in the closing sheet.

We recognize, that according to that setting, the respective accounts can be planned either by changing amount or closing amount. The assumption we made regarding the sample data and accounts is:

  • Planned by CLOSING_INPUT > Valuation accounts
  • Planned by CHANGING_INPUT > operational accounts
  • Planned by DRIVER_INPUT > data by accounts receivables or accounts payables

We analyzed, that both calculation sheets together give the complete balance sheet.

The questions are now:

  1. What is the difference between the calculation methods DRIVER_INPUT, CLOSING_INPUT and CHANGING_INPUT? Are our assumptions correct?
  2. How is DRIVER_INPUT calculated or entered?
  3. Why are the accounts labeled as it is with the corresponding Calc. Method (or empty)? How is this to interpret?
  4. How are both Balance Sheets technically connected? What is the business intention behind those both views? How should those be read?

Thank you for you explanations and best regards!

entry.title: Re: IDoc Retry Not Processing New Segments After Enhancem...
entry.content:
To resolve the issue where reprocessed IDocs (via BD87/Fiori) ignore the new segment Z1INV_DETAIL, follow these steps:

1. Check Existing IDoc Instances

  • Old IDocs retain their original structure: If the failing IDocs were generated before adding the segment, they will not include Z1INV_DETAIL. Reprocessing (BD87) sends the existing IDoc data, not a new one.

  • Solution:

    • Cancel/archive old IDocs and regenerate new ones from the source transaction (e.g., FB70 for invoices).

    • Verify new IDocs via WE02 or WE05 to confirm the segment exists.

2. Clear IDoc Metadata Cache

  • IDoc structures are cached in memory. Even after activation, old definitions may persist.

  • Clear the cache:

    • Run transaction BD21 (Reset IDoc Type Cache) or execute program RBDCACHE_ACTIVE_DELETE.

    • Restart the IDoc processing service (transaction SM58 or SMICM if using HTTP).

3. Verify Partner Profile (WE20)

  • Ensure the outbound partner profile includes the new segment:

    • Go to WE20 > Partner Type > Output > Message Type ZINVOIC02.

    • Check "Basic Type" matches the updated ZINVOIC02.

    • Confirm the "Outbound Options" do not strip custom segments (e.g., no "reduced IDoc" flag).

4. Process Code Configuration (WE42/WE41)

  • The process code (assigned in WE42) must link to the updated IDoc type:

    • Check WE42 (Output Determination) for the relevant process code (e.g., ZIV).

    • Ensure it references the correct IDoc type ZINVOIC02 in NACE or WE41.

5. Check Custom Function Module/EDI Configuration

  • If using a custom outbound function module (e.g., IDOC_OUTPUT_INVOIC), ensure it includes logic to populate the new segment.

  • For EDI subsystems (e.g., SAP PI/PO), reprocess the IDoc through the subsystem to ensure it picks the new structure.

6. Relevant SAP Notes

  • SAP Note 314635: IDoc structure changes not reflected due to caching.

  • SAP Note 218716: Issues with BD87 reprocessing old IDoc structures.

  • SAP Note 1882373: Partner profile settings causing segment truncation.

Debugging Steps

  • Use WE19 to create a test IDoc with the new segment and compare its control record (EDIDC) and data records (EDIDD) with the reprocessed IDoc (via WE02).

  • Check table EDIDC (field IDOCTYP) and EDIDD (field SEGNAM) to confirm the new segment exists.

  • Enable IDoc tracing via WE47 to monitor segment generation during reprocessing.

The issue arises because existing IDoc instances retain their original structure, and reprocessing tools (BD87/Fiori) resend the old data. Clear the IDoc cache, regenerate new IDocs, and validate partner profiles/process codes. If the legacy system still rejects the segment, confirm its mapping accepts Z1INV_DETAIL in the inbound interface.

entry.title: Re: BW Bridge: In what schema can I find table TFACS?
entry.content:
Hi @mitko1994 ,

Since BW Bridge runs on the BTP ABAP Environment, the integration and access to the factory calendar work differently compared to traditional ABAP systems. Accessing DDIC tables like TFACS directly is not allowed anymore, as these are not released for use in ABAP Cloud.

Instead, SAP provides public APIs and CDS views that allow you to access factory calendar data in a compliant way. Since you're accessing it via AMDP, I recommend checking this documentation:
👉 Accessing Holiday and Factory Calendar Tables Using VDM-Compliant CDS Views
There you'll find the list of released CDS views, such as I_PublicHolidayCalendarBasic.

Alternatively, you could also move the calendar logic outside of BW Bridge and implement it directly in SAP Datasphere. A colleague of mine wrote a helpful blog post that might guide you through this approach:
👉 Combining Date and Factory Calendar Data in SAP Datasphere

Hope this helps!

Best regards,

David

entry.title: Re: OPEX Cost centre allocation - Advanced formula clarif...
entry.content:
Dear @praveen_kumar334 

the logic of Allocate CCtr Expenses with Account Grouping is the following:
the allocated expenses are assigned to the accounts maintained in Allocation Account property of dimension SAP_FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT.

Please note the following:

  • If an allocation account ismaintained for an account, thesystem posts to this allocationaccount.
  • If no allocation account ismaintained, but an allocationaccount exists for the account '# -Unassigned', then this account isused as default allocation account.
  • If the two cases mentioned abovedo not apply, the original accountis used, so the account is not changed at all.

So what happens in the code:
First we're aggregating the costs over activity type and partner objects, but we're keeping the primary account.
We aggregate the allocation weights over partner cost center.
Then we can calculate the costs for each primary account.

Once this is done, we can allocate thoses costs (Line 58 in the code)
The formula ((xyz) *0 +1) is equal 1 in case data is available on the specific account and null in case there is no data.
So the first part
(RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "AMOUNT", [d/SAP_FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT] = [d/SAP_FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT].[p/ALLOCATION_ACCOUNT]) * 0 + 1)
is checking if the data was allocated to the account specified in property p/ALLOCATION_ACCOUNT of the account dimension.
If there is no valid account set in this property, the result is null.

For the second part of the formula
(RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = #COST) * 0 + 1)
the result is 1

In our example the result is then 0

DATA([d/Measures] = "AMOUNT", [d/SAP_FI_IFP_CODEBITCREDITCODE] = #IS_ALLOC_ACCOUNT_VALID)
= (RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "AMOUNT", [d/SAP_FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT] = [d/SAP_FI_IFP_GLACCOUNT].[p/ALLOCATION_ACCOUNT]) * 0 + 1)
+ (RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = #COST) * 0 + 1) - 1
0 = null +1 -1

If there is a valid allocation account maintained in property p/ALLOCATION_ACCOUNT of the account dimension, then the result would be 1 (1+1 -1)

Regarding your second question:
The allocation allocates the costs of a cost center to the specified partner cost centers, based on the weights maintained in the SAP_FI_IFP_IM_OPEX_PlanAdmin),CCtrAllocation Weights tab.

When using our sample data, this means, that the costs of cost center Build. & Maint (US) will be allocated evenly (50:50) to partner cost centers Manufacturing 1 (US) and Manufacturing 2 (US).

Does this answer your questions?

Maybe our demo script will give you some further insights.

Best regards
Susanne

entry.title: Re: Configuration for sending Purchase order in XML format to a middleware using OPD
entry.content: Dear Expert,

entry.title: Re: Manage Purchase Requisitions filtering
entry.content: Helo,

entry.title: Re: Notification to HR when Onboardee is in MPH
entry.content:
Hi inepre1,

You can follow this approach from ashg in the community posts and customize per your requirement. When creating a custom email category ensure you use the same content builder in the correlating custom email template.

https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-successfactors-onboarding-user-group/custom-email-notification-onboarding-manage-pending-hires-personal-data/m-p/13940793/thread-id/578/emcs_t/S2h8ZW1haWx8ZGlnZXN0X25vdGlmaWNhdGlvbnxNM05ZUzMwRFhUUkpJTHwtMXxPVEhFUlN8aEs

Regards

Frans

Thanks

entry.title: Navigating the Global Landscape with Cloud ERP: Insights from PwC's Journey
entry.content:
In a rapidly evolving business environment, organizations are constantly seeking to unlock more productivity, efficiency, and innovation. One proven catalyst for this progress is Enterprise Resource Planning or ERP systems run on public cloud infrastructures. A cloud-based ERP system not only helps to streamline operations and ensure seamless data flow across various business functions but also brings in scalability, reducing the strain on in-house IT infrastructures.

At the nexus of ERP technology, international business, and regulatory compliance, sits a critical concept: Localization. It’s an often-overlooked aspect that can transform the business' functionality globally.

Expanding Horizons: Unlocking Global Potential with Cloud ERP Localization

Most companies operate across multiple jurisdictions with diverse tax guidelines, legal frameworks, and statutory requirements. Ensuring compliance with local standards while maintaining a globally uniform ERP system can be a considerable challenge. That's where ERP localization comes in.

Localization refers to the adaptation of an ERP system to cater to the legislative, cultural, operational, and market requirements of a particular country or a region. As part of ERP implementation, localization accounts for languages, taxation rules, fiscal obligations, and various other national considerations.

Mr. Marc Hoessels, Managing Director of PwC, explains the profound impact of localization on cloud ERP systems. Over the past eight years, Mr. Hoessels’ primary role has been to ensure tax-and-legal compliance for their global clients. He has successfully embraced localization, leading to broader adoption and substantial benefits for their clients. Mr. Hoessels adds, "With 50,000 professionals worldwide in 150+ countries, implementing a centralized approach called an acceleration package helps us comply with local requirements across different jurisdictions smoothly."

Why Choose Cloud ERP?

Cloud-based ERP solutions offer numerous benefits that make them appealing to businesses across various sectors. According to IDC’s SAP Global Future of Trust & Security Survey, the top reasons for moving to a cloud ERP system include better security, compliance, privacy, and risk posture (45%), and better alignment with company values and ESG initiatives (34%). Furthermore, cloud-based ERP users report much greater trust in their systems compared to on-prem ERP users, with 31% of cloud-based ERP users reporting 100% trust in their ERP provider.

Mr. Michael Rasch, a partner at PwC's German practice and the leader of PwC’s Global SAP Alliance, provides further insight into why PwC chose SAP's cloud ERP. Mr. Rasch shares, "PwC is an SAP user itself. We are one of the largest clients of SAP when it comes to the public cloud edition. Before we moved to the S/4HANA journey with SAP, we did a calculation on how many different accounting systems we had around the globe. We figured out we had more than 50 different accounting systems, which really made no sense for a professional service firm. That's when we decided to adopt SAP's 'golden standard' for our finance-related data."

PwC started implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition in central European countries like Germany, Austria, Switzerland, Belgium, the Netherlands, and Turkey. According to Mr. Rasch, "We are now rolling it out to the U.S., Japan, and other large markets. Using the public cloud also helps us limit variances and ensures compliance with specific national laws, using tools like SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)."

The public cloud ERP's compatibility with functional localization facilitates adherence to laws, regulations, and standards in various jurisdictions, all while using a single, unified system. Therefore, alongside these benefits, organizations can harness additional productivity gains through ERP localization.

"Using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition helps us streamline our accounting systems across our 156 firms," Mr. Rasch explains. "It ensures that the larger entities in our network derive significant value from a standardized and efficient system."

These insights from the IDC’s SAP Global Future of Trust & Security Survey and Mr. Rasch's experience illustrate the significant advantages of cloud ERP solutions, including increased security, compliance, and trust in the systems used.

From Experience to Expertise

PwC leverages their own experience as a customer of cloud ERP to enhance the services they provide to their clients. This dual perspective allows them to understand the challenges and solutions intimately. PwC approaches localization with a clear understanding of a client's operations and the jurisdictions they are active in.

PwC provides a centralized approach to localization through regional expertise. Teams across Asia, the Middle East, Africa, Europe, and the Americas leverage their local knowledge of SAP solutions to aid clients' global transitions seamlessly. Mr. Hoessels notes, "The new SAP public cloud offerings, such as time-dependent tax code stamping, have proven very useful. We maintain these rate changes as a managed service, ensuring clients stay compliant with local tax regulations."

Summary

Embracing a public cloud-based ERP system, coupled with effective localization, can substantially bolster operational efficiency, regulatory compliance, and global scalability. The partnership between PwC and SAP exemplifies the power of combining advanced ERP solutions with deep localization to navigate the global business landscape effectively.

Mr.Rasch reiterates their growth and success in this partnership, having seen their practice grow around 20% year over year in the past three years, driven by the robust needs of clients and the compelling offerings from SAP. With this dynamic collaboration, PwC leads the way in leveraging cloud ERP and localization to drive transformative success for its clients globally.

Learn about Localization Today:

SAP Community for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Localization

Localization Capabilities on sap.com

SAP Community for Localization 

Building Better Trust in Business with Cloud ERP Investments

entry.title: Re: Incorrect Cost (FIFO) When Returning
entry.content:
Hi,

from your screenshots I would say that this is due to rounding. Under Administration => System Initialisation => General Settings, tab Display you can check this, but it seems that Prices are set to 5 decimals, and Amounts to 2 decimals. This means that any price can be shown with 5 decimals, but final calculation of Total Amount needs to be rounded to 2 decimals and is saved as such.

BR,

Matija

entry.title: Re: How to Update Material Status on Sales Org 2 Tab in ECC
entry.content: Go to mm17, select MARC table, click on Fields and select field MMSTA and execute. Now you can try to change. Incidentally, in standard, this MMSTA field will not be in Sales Org2 view as you mentioned.

entry.title: Re: Integrating Data Import Job into Multi Actions
entry.content:
Great Summary,

But a quick question. If the multi-action task is created in the calendar with a recurrence, then several tasks appear in the calendar.

Assuming we want to run this import job weekly, there would be 52 tasks in the calendar, which is really confusing.

So I thought we could group the whole thing with a superordinate process. Now the surprise -> This is not possible in the SAP Analytics Cloud. 

Does anyone know a reason for this behavior? Or a solution how a multi-action task with many repetitions does not fill the whole calendar?
SAP Analytics Cloud for planning @SarauShen

entry.title: Re: Top 10 SAP Cloud ALM News March 2025
entry.content:
Hello Marbeth,

That is very good news. However, I would also be interested in the possibility of adding a project track landscape so that we can develop two different projects for a single production system. In my case, we have a project track with development, test, and pre-prod systems, and then a maintenance track with development, test, pre-prod, and production systems. At this moment, we are managing the entire project through FocusedBuild.

entry.title: Re: SAP Subscription Billing - Data Migration
entry.content:

what kind of master data to you specifically have in mind?

Best
Carsten

entry.title: Re: Joule speaks ABAP!
entry.content:
Hi @Basant_Joshi 

currently, Joule is not available in BTP Trial. If you have a productive instance of SAP BTP ABAP Environment or correspondingly SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can purchase (for free) the corresponding material. The configuration in your system will then be triggered, and once completed, you can use Joule in ABAP Development Tools for this instance.

Best regards,
Alexander

entry.title: Cannot read property 'businessPartner' of undefined
entry.content:
I am currently trying to change the credit limit and resumsion date for certain business partners and credid segment via api, the data is read from an excel file, this works fine but I get an error message after the 76 entry.

 

    "file": "bundle.js",
    "exception": "irpa_core.error.Error",
    "stack": "Error: Cannot read property 'businessPartner' of undefined\n

Do you have an idea what the problem is, it is strange that it stops exactly after the 76 entry?

entry.title: Re: BW Bridge: In what schema can I find table TFACS?
entry.content: Thanks, David. I will probably proceed with combining the calendar data in Datasphere as per the blog.

entry.title: Re: Select multiple values on a picklist in the job requi...
entry.content:
hello,

 

I have the same situation with a field and my xml format is below. You can try to add multiselect=true

<field-definition id="mfield3" type="picklist" required="true" custom="false" multiselect="true">
<field-label><![CDATA[Candidates from:]]></field-label>
<field-label lang="en_US"><![CDATA[Candidates from:]]></field-label>
<field-description><![CDATA[Select the source of Candidates]]></field-description>
<picklist-id>candidatesFrom</picklist-id>
</field-definition>

Hope this help.

entry.title: Re: No service found for namespace '', name 'API_OUTBOUND...
entry.content:
Hi Kaito2,

I think that You have error in typing.

You should have : API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV_0002:v2

https://api.sap.com/api/API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV_0002/overview

Best Regards 

Łukasz

Purchase Requisition will be created.

Soma

entry.title: Re: Assertion Faileure 201503

Starting database "TEST" (C:\Temp\TEST.db) at Fri Mar 28 2025 08:21
Database recovery in progress
Last checkpoint at Tue Mar 18 2025 19:49
Checkpoint log...
Opening dbspace 'TEST' in file 'TEST.DBS' for database 'TEST'
Transaction log: TEST.log...
*** ERROR *** Assertion failed: 201503 (17.0.9.4913)[TEST]
Record 0x1 not present on page 0x0:0x50475

entry.title: HANA upgrade
entry.content:
Hello Team,

As part of SAP's continuous maintenance cycles and in accordance with their notification, we upgrade the SAP HANA Cloud Database to the latest available version. 

There are two subaccounts in which we are trying to add the application "SAP HANA CLOUD" under subscription, but there is no "tools" plan.

Could you please guide us on it.

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: HANA upgrade
entry.content:
Hello,

Please refer to:

https://me.sap.com/notes/3303080 and the official SAP HANA Cloud documentation for more information:

https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud/sap-hana-cloud-administration-guide/subscribing-to-sap-hana-cloud-administration-tools

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: Restore deleted chart of accounts
entry.content:
Did you accidentally use a global delete option in the menu, such as F7 + shift-F2 ?

Was this data stored in a released transport request that you could import back into the current system, or transported to another system where you could re-export it back into the current system in a copy transport request (in case too many objects in the original request) ?

entry.title: Re: In SAC System Overview--> Authorization Missing Options
entry.content: Can you share a screenshot as an example of what you are looking for and what you think is currently missing?

entry.title: Re: GROW with SAP Learning Roadmap
entry.content:
Hi IkEgh,

I suggest to start learning from: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/implementing-sap-s-4-hana-cloud-public-edition

First certificate should be C_S4CPB - https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-implementation-consultant-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition#how-to-prepare

Next according to You LoB You should choose: 

* FI - https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-implementation-consultant-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-financial-accounting

* SD - https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-implementation-consultant-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-sales

* MM https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-implementation-consultant-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-sourcing-and-procurement

I Hope this will help You.

Best regards 

Łukasz

entry.title: Re: How to concatenate value in Global Variable
entry.content:
Hi @Pyae_Phyo_Thwe ,

When you write the global variable, if you have marked the flag "Global scope", you can read the variable from any iflow.

Regards,

Roberto

entry.title: Re: Repointing Universe from Oracle to Snowflake
entry.content:
Hi there,

to assess the scale of your efforts:

  1. Take a copy of your universe
  2. Create Snowflake connection
  3. Change connection on your universe copy from #1 above to the Snowflake connection
  4. Run universe integrity checks
  5. Integrity check will highlight changes in tables and Oracle specific SQL Syntax that will need to rewritten in Snowflake SQL

regards

James

https://www.jemstar.ie/

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Composite Roles in launchpad content and layout tools
entry.content:
Hello all, 

please be aware to not maintain composite role menu (manually) in addition to the role menus of the single roles, that contain SAP Fiori Business Catalogs. The App Finder will not retrieve catalogs from contained single roles, if composite role menu is maintained. Probably best option would be to leave the role menu of the (Fiori) composite roles empty.

See SAP Note 2538787 - Catalogs in roles that are part of a composite role cannot be seen by end users - Fiori Launchpad

Best regards

Simon

entry.title: Tricentis XScan is failing to scan a page on Microsoft Edge
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I'm trying to work with Tricentis Test Automation for Cloud ALM, and I'm having trouble with XScan and the Tricentis Automation Extension for Edge.

When I try to scan a Web browser, I select the appropriate option on Tosca Cloud.

This launches XScan, which starts up correctly (as far as I can tell).

I then select which application and click Scan.

The first strange thing happens. A popup asking me for the extension shows up, even if the extension is installed and up to date.

I will click No and now I have an intermittent connection displayed on the extension.

Sometimes it's green, sometimes it shows failure.

If I click to Scan Screen anyway on XScan.

I get the following error.

Any help would be much appreciated 🙂

Thank you,
Bruno

entry.title: Analysis of the name sequence when double-clicking on the contact person's PF in a sales order
entry.content:
Analysis of the name sequence when double-clicking on the contact person's partner function in a sales order.

Sometimes, the sequence of names displays the customer's name first and the contact person's name last.

 

Sometimes, the sequence of names displays the contact person’s name first and the customer's name last.

 


Analysis: Set break point at FM SD_PARTNER_ADDR_DIALOG_INTERN, in this case, system then call FM ADDRESS_INTO_PRINTFORM.

 

The name sequence get determined by each address layout. When the address layout is 011, we can see that the customer's name is listed first.

 

When the address layout is 022, we can see that the contact person’s name is listed first.

 

For different address layout, you can refer to this help portal. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f86dc2eb1f8b48c880a7607213104b27/6dee8af7a26e482388c895f2b9f1e605.html?version=2502.500

 

The address layout key is determined by the receiver's country. By using transaction code OY07, we can identify the country for which the layout key is defined.

entry.title: Re: EOIO in SAP CI Using JMS - Exclusive Mode
entry.content:
Hi Alex,

I also tried to implement a PoC scenario with XI sender adapter using "Handled by Integration Flow" option. When ECC sender system triggers an EOIO message to CPI following error occurs in ECC and CPI monitoring:

1. ECC ZZ_BC_PI_MONITOR:

2. CPI message monitor:

 

The IFlow remains infinite in processing state.

In ERP ECC I configured an interface-specific connection in SM59 (HTTP destination to CPI endpoint) and SXMB_ADM (Sender/receiver definition, Integration Engine Configuration -> IS_URL parameter).
Above error only appears when "Handled by Integration Flow" option is selected. When I switch to "At Least Once" option with JMS queue the XI message is successfully processed by CPI IFlow.

Do you have any idea what could be the error root cause?

BR,
Tobias Miller

entry.title: In SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud what global parameters - Y20 is used for?
entry.content:
Dear All,
I am trying to implement Group Reporting in SAP S/HANA Public Cloud based on the 1SG test script.
I am currently at the the preliminary steps. According to the test script we need to set up the Global Parameters. The script says use the version ’Y20’ without any explanation. Why do I need to choose Y20? What does it do? What difference does it make? Why shouldn’t I use Y10 instead?

Thank you and best regards,
Reka

entry.title: Re: Separate authorization for maintaining and deleting b...
entry.content:
Hi ChristopherWan,

there is no possibility to enhancement authorization. 

Best Regards

Łukasz

entry.title: Release of SAP Disclosure Management Stack 2202
entry.content:
We are pleased to announce the release of Stack 2202, bringing a series of enhancements and legal updates designed to improve your experience and facilitate more efficient operations. In the https://me.sap.com/notes/3585594, you will find fixes in various components in the new release. Below are the key highlights and details:

DM CLIENT 10.1: 

  • Feature Enhancement Sustainability Control Tower metrics:
    It is now possible to add metrics to briefing books, allowing you to execute the same metrics with different variables using different briefing books or execute metrics with the same variables without having to enter them multiple times.
    Since the concept had significantly changed, migration of existing data links cannot be performed and they must be updated or reset.

INTERST. XWAND TAX ED&IN CR 13

  • New Version: A new release of the Interstage XWand Toolkit by Fujitsu is available. Version 13.7.3 includes native support for XBRL Calculations 1.1 and EFM (Edgar Filer Manual vol. 2) v72.
    • Installation Note: Please uninstall the previous version before installing this new one to ensure proper functionality.

IXBRL CREATION PLUGIN 10.1: 

  • Feature Enhancement Package Creation: You can now instantly create packages by setting a report configuration parameter. This is necessary to fully validate certain scenarios like ESEF. To enable it, set report parameter 'Create Package' to 'Yes'.

  • Feature Enhancement Fact ID: For better analysis, an ID attribute with a unique value for each tagged fact can be included by a report configuration parameter in the iXBRL report. To enable it, set report parameter 'Add ID to facts' to 'Yes'.

IXBRL PACKAGE PLUGIN 10.1: 

  • Discontinuation: Due to the integration of the package function into the IXBRL Creation Plugin, the Package plugin is no longer needed. An official discontinuation announcement for the plugin will be available soon. Therefore, the IXBRL PACKAGE PLUGIN 10.1 must not be used in combination with Stack 2202.
  • After installation of Stack 2202:
    1. Copy all parameter values from the plugin 'XBRL - Mapping inline - Package' to their corresponding ones in 'XBRL - Mapping inline - Create'.
    2. Set the parameter 'Create package' to 'Yes'.
    Additionally:
    • Remove the plugin 'XBRL - Mapping inline - Package' from the existing report type, or create a new report type.
    • If you create a new report type, ensure it is assigned to the XBRL report.

XBRL TEMPL CREATION PLG 10.1: xBRL-CSV instance creation

  • Feature Enhancement: xBRL-CSV instance creation for DORA reporting with the EBA 4.0 taxonomy is now supported. DORA (Digital Operational Resilience Act) is an upcoming enhancement of the European XBRL business scenarios. It will be the first to require CSV files to be sent to the regulator.

entry.title: Re: Nested IF Statement Comparing Versions
entry.content:
@Freddie 

logic: Write a "1" to Measure = Mismatch_Indicator for every member in "Site" Dimenstion if sales are above zero in Version = Public.Z AND there are no sales in Version = Public.X. 

 

So If you have 100 in version Z and nothing ( null) or 0 in version X for site members you want to flag these

One approach could be you test the condition like below

Value in ver Z + Value in Ver X = Value in Version Z

100+ null or 0= 100

IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Net_Sales_ext", [d/Version] = "public.Z", [d/DIM1] = "#", [d/DIM2] = "#") +

RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Net_Sales_ext", [d/Version] = "public.X") =  RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = "Net_Sales_ext", [d/Version] = "public.Z", [d/DIM1] = "#", [d/DIM2] = "#")

THEN
DATA([d/Measures] = "Mismatch_Indicator") = 1

 

You have to be careful about other dimensions which you have not mentioned in the post.

The problem in your approach is IF condition filters the dimension context. So your second if will not even get triggered as the dimension context of first if will not be true for second IF. Either put both IF test in AND condition or use above approach.

Rough coding like below

IF RESULTLOOKUP(ver Z) >0 AND  (RESULTLOOKUP (VerX)=0 OR RESULTLOOKUP(ver X) is NULL)

THEN DATA(indicator)=1

ENDIF

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: BW Bridge: In what schema can I find table TFACS?
entry.content:
Hi,

do you mean for a solution within BW Bridge or in SAP Datasphere?

In Datasphere, the approach is documented in the blog I shared, where you can join the Datasphere view SAP.TIME.M_TIME_DIMENSION with the S/4HANA view I_FACTORYCALWORKINGDAYSPERYR to get the desired working day calculations.

In BW Bridge it's a bit different. You do have access to an ABAP API, but this would mean stepping outside of SQL/AMDP logic. You can use the following API:

  • Interface: IF_FHC_FCAL_RUNTIME

  • Method: CALC_WORKINGDAYS_BETWEEN_DATES
    More details can be found here:
    👉 https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/rel-comp-factory-calendar#reading-factory-calendar-related-information

If you're looking for a pure SQL-based solution in BW Bridge using AMDP, there currently isn’t a released CDS view equivalent to I_FACTORYCALWORKINGDAYSPERYR from S/4HANA. You’d likely need to build your own logic by joining views like I_FactoryCalendarBasic with standard date views such as I_CalendarDate.

You might also find some inspiration or examples in this SAP Community thread:
👉 https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/wanted-i-calendardate-like-abap-cds-view-for-work-days-of-a-factory/qaq-p/12532183

If it's an option for your use case, I would still recommend handling this logic in Datasphere, as it's much more flexible for these types of calculations.

Best regards,
David

entry.title: Re: Create Custom BADI and not implement all methods
entry.content:
Hello,

as I had this problem as well, through its been a few years since this was asked, and the answer from Sandra Rossi was rather useless for the actual solution, here is the solution I found: 

In the Interface Methode you can either add "DEFAULT IGNORE" after the method name in eclipse or do the same by going to the method properties and selecting "Define runtime behavior when method is not implemented" -> "Emulate empty method implementation".

After doing this, the BAdI Implementation does not need all the method implemented anymore.

entry.title: Re: How can i revert back to an old version of a universe?
entry.content: How to retrieve a previous Business Object universe version

entry.title: In S/4HANA Public Cloud why do we select a period if we want to do consolidation for the full year?

It is still in the Set Global Parameters Configuration?
Also, when I save this in the configuration why isn’t a transport request created?



Thank you and best regards,

entry.title: Re: PM Order - Functional Location and Equipments on Oper...
entry.content:
Greetings,

this is read only information which is populated by data from notification which was created from the operation. 

Try creating a notification, maintain the reference techical object information and observe how this data will be populated.

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Re: How to upload G/L master data template in SAP S4HANA...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

The Starter System in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition has certain restrictions compared to the DEV system, as it is mainly used for evaluation and initial configuration rather than full-scale development. If you don't see the "Export Worklist" option in the Manage Operating Chart of Accounts app in the Starter System, here are a few possible reasons and workarounds:

Possible Reasons:

1. Limited Functionalities in Starter System:

The Starter System may not support full data migration functionalities like the DEV system.

Some options, including "Export Worklist", might be disabled or not available in the Starter System.

 

2. Missing Business Role Assignment:

The role assigned to your user in the Starter System might not have the necessary authorization.

Check if your user has the required role (e.g., SAP_BR_GL_ACCOUNTANT or similar).

 

3. Differences in System Versions or Configuration:

The DEV and Starter Systems might have slight variations in features due to updates or configurations.

 

Possible Workarounds:

1. Check for Alternative Apps

Try using these Fiori apps instead:

Migrate Your Data (for legacy data migration)

Import Financial Plan Data (to check if you can import via another method)

Manage G/L Account Master Data (to manually check and verify uploaded data)

 

2. Manually Upload the G/L Master Data

If the Export Worklist option is unavailable, try manually uploading your G/L master data:

Use the "Manage Your Solution" App

a. Open Manage Your Solution in Fiori.

b. Search for G/L Master Data or Finance Configuration.

c. Look for an upload option or migration functionality.

 

Use the "Migrate Your Data" App (Data Migration Cockpit)

a. Open Migrate Your Data in Fiori.

b. Select the appropriate migration object (G/L Accounts).

c. Upload the G/L master data template.

 

3. Use the API Approach (If Feasible)

If SAP provides an API for G/L master data uploads, you might be able to use an OData or SOAP API to upload the data programmatically.

 

4. Contact SAP Support

If you suspect a system limitation, raise an SAP support ticket to confirm whether "Export Worklist" is deliberately restricted in the Starter System.

 

I hope this will help answering your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Scripting Tracker - Development Tool for SAP GUI Scripting
entry.content:
Hi @Stefan-Schnell ,

Need your advice on where I can download script tracker tool.

I checked your website: https://tracker.stschnell.de/ however unfortunately I got this message below. It said end of life 😞

entry.title: Re: unable to drill down in a Crystal report chart
entry.content: thanks for continuing to comment here Don. The weird thing is that if we go to the old server that this new one would take over from, in the same browser (different tab) on the same desktop, the drill down from the charts in the old system is fine. Could be permissions/trust to the new site/URL as you say, I'll ask IT at my customer to look into those settings

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps - Renaming Apps
entry.content:
@Pekka_Aaltonen 

Dear Pekka, 

It is not even possible to copy an app presently from the menu close to app icon. (see screenshot)

Could you please check and also raise the level for the upload of app file to 500MB ( or 400)? I still get error status code 500 (see screenshot). Thank you!

Best regards

Chantal

entry.title: Re: Exploring the Banner in the New Learning Page of SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

We would like to customize the white color of each specific course in the section shown in the following image:

could you help me understand if it is feasible and guide me through the configuration steps?"

Regards,
maria

entry.title: Is there any recommendation for using @cds.persistence.journal?
entry.content:
I’m looking for guidance on when (or if) it's recommended to use @CDS.persistence.journal.
From what I understand, a potential drawback is that it might not play well with multitenancy, when tenants want to use custom extensions.

 

If this is not the case, should this annotation be used as default, since it seems very advantageous.
Or are there any other drawbacks that I'm not aware of?

entry.title: Re: Generating Adobe Form Using RAP framework OData Services
entry.content:
Hi,

How did you point the XSD file to your custom OData service? I downloaded the XSD file from custom template in Maintain Form Templates app but the fields generated in the XSD file are completely wrong and don't seem to be relevant to the OData service I created. Could you please advise?

entry.title: standard filters not working in internal career portal
entry.content:
Hi,

Please, standard filters (business unit and division) are not working in the internal career portal even if we've activated the business unit object in the internal career search settings. Any help please?

entry.title: Re: CAP with Fiori Elements: Configure Multiple Views on List Report Tables
entry.content:
Hi Ajith,

Unfortunately, nothing works here any longer except "cap-fe-lr-op-actions".

Typical errors:

entry.title: Re: No service found for namespace '', name 'API_OUTBOUND...
entry.content: Thank you for suggesting me that possibility, but I confirmed the url entered correctly. I will investigate the possibility the EDI tool failed to encode it and made it a capital letter. Other than that, do you have any idea related to this error such as some necesarry authentication else?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA direct connectivity with Event Mesh in Integration Suite
entry.content: We have resolved this issue. In SMICM we found that it is looking for Issuer/Root certificate ISRG-X1 and R10 which was downloaded from amqp host url https://<your-account>.eu10.a.eventmesh.integration.cloud.sap and added it in SSL Standard along with Anonymous.

entry.title: Re: Purchase Requisition created from Service Order
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, when a service order with multiple external items is released, purchase requisitions are created for each service item. However, there is no standard configuration available Purchase Requisition for service order with external items to change the document type and number range specifically for these purchase requisitions derived from service orders with external items. This behavior is part of the expected functionality of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and currently, there isn’t a direct way to modify the document type or number range specifically for these purchase requisitions.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Map format data field FPAYHX-REF01
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

 

FPAYHX is a payment medium structure used in SAP's payment formats (e.g., SEPA, ACH, wire transfers).
 
The field REF01 usually stores reference information, such as invoice numbers, payment reference IDs, or customer/vendor identifiers.
 
I hope this will help you answer your question.
 
Best regards,
Jeremy
